title: Application hit RDS max_connections during traffic spike
symptoms: Application logs show 'FATAL: too many connections for role app'
RDS DatabaseConnections metric is flat at the maximum
Users see 5xx responses during peak hours
root_cause: Each pod opens its own connection pool and traffic doubled
Long-lived Lambda concurrency spikes created new connections faster than RDS could recycle
No connection pooler (RDS Proxy or PgBouncer) in front of the database
diagnosis: Check Performance Insights → Top SQL for hanging sessions
SELECT count(*) FROM pg_stat_activity GROUP BY state;
Look at CloudWatch DatabaseConnections and CPUUtilization
fix: Introduce RDS Proxy in front of the database:

```bash
aws rds create-db-proxy \
  --db-proxy-name app-proxy \
  --engine-family POSTGRESQL \
  --auth AuthScheme=SECRETS,SecretArn=$SECRET \
  --role-arn $ROLE_ARN
```
Lower the application pool size per pod and raise RDS max_connections in the parameter group
Kill stuck sessions: `SELECT pg_terminate_backend(pid) FROM pg_stat_activity WHERE state = 'idle in transaction' AND state_change < now() - interval '10 min';`
prevention: Load test the pool size × replica count against max_connections
Adopt RDS Proxy for all serverless workloads

title: Application receives 403 AccessDenied when reading S3 after IAM change
symptoms: boto3 or aws s3 cp returns 403 AccessDenied
Worked fine before the last IAM policy update
root_cause: Overly restrictive bucket policy conflicts with IAM role
Missing kms:Decrypt permission for the SSE-KMS key
S3 Block Public Access or VPC endpoint policy blocking the call
diagnosis: Copy the CloudTrail entry and paste into the IAM policy simulator
Inspect the VPC endpoint policy if using PrivateLink
fix: Grant the role permission to the KMS key:
Align the bucket policy principal ARN with the actual role used at runtime
Re-deploy with the fresh credentials and validate via `aws sts get-caller-identity`
prevention: Enforce a break-glass review for bucket policy changes
Use Access Analyzer to detect unintended public access
Write integration tests that read/write a canary object on deploy
